steps.title: Find f(3) using the functional equation
steps.content:
We are given that $f(cx) = x - 7$ for all values of $x$. To find $f(3)$, we need to determine what value of $x$ makes $cx = 3$. Solving for $x$: $x = \frac{3}{c}$. Now substitute this into the functional equation:
$$f(3) = f(cx) = x - 7 = \frac{3}{c} - 7$$

steps.title: Solve for c using f(3) = 31
steps.content:
We are told that $f(3) = 31$. From the previous step, we have $f(3) = \frac{3}{c} - 7$. Setting these equal:
$$\frac{3}{c} - 7 = 31$$
Add 7 to both sides:
$$\frac{3}{c} = 38$$
Multiply both sides by $c$ and divide by 38:
$$c = \frac{3}{38} \approx 0.0789$$
